ours.title: Harlem River Drive
ours.label: Roulette
ours.albumDNA: Puerto Rican piano legend Eddie Palmieri's genre-shattering album fuses salsa, soul, funk, and jazz into a revolutionary Nuyorican sound. The extended jams build hypnotic grooves that blend clave rhythms with wah-wah guitar and horn arrangements of breathtaking power. A milestone in Latin music that broke down the walls between salsa and jazz-funk. Palmieri's visionary fusion of Afro-Caribbean and African-American musical traditions anticipated decades of cross-cultural experimentation.

ours.title: Mystic Brew
ours.label: Blue Note
ours.albumDNA: Organist Ronnie Foster's 1972 album Mystic Brew draws from the soul jazz tradition, released on Blue Note. The music draws on soul jazz and jazz-funk influences. Blue Note, under Alfred Lion and Francis Wolff, was the definitive hard bop label, with Rudy Van Gelder engineering many of its classic sessions. Bridged soul jazz and the emerging funk movement. Its influence on hip-hop cannot be overstated.

ours.title: The Real Book
ours.label: Bootleg
ours.albumDNA: The Real Book is an album by Various Artists, released in 1975 on Bootleg. The record sits in the bebop / contemporary jazz tradition. Notable tracks include "All The Things You Are" and "Autumn Leaves".

ours.title: Juice
ours.label: RVC
ours.albumDNA: A jazz-funk outing from 1977, Juice features guitarist Ryo Kawasaki recording for RVC. The program includes "Juice", "Raisins", and "Bamboo Child". A cult classic of Japanese jazz-funk. Kawasaki's guitar synthesizer pioneering influenced later electronic jazz.

ours.title: Mellow Dream
ours.label: Trio Records
ours.albumDNA: The follow-up to Scenery finds Fukui in an even more confident and exploratory mood. The trio swings hard through originals and standards, with Fukui's rapid-fire runs and delicate ballad work revealing the full range of his artistry. Confirmed Fukui's debut was no fluke. Along with Scenery, it documented one of Japan's most gifted pianists at his creative peak in Sapporo's intimate jazz scene.

ours.title: Tetterettet
ours.label: ICP
ours.albumDNA: The Instant Composers Pool Orchestra, led by Dutch drummer Han Bennink and pianist Misha Mengelberg, brings Dadaist humor and theatrical absurdity to free jazz. The music careens between composed passages, free improvisation, and outright chaos with gleeful abandon. Defined the Dutch approach to free improvisation, which embraced humor, theatricality, and Monk-influenced composition alongside total freedom. The ICP's playful spirit was a counterweight to the earnestness of much European free music.

ours.label: Unknown
ours.albumDNA: Chet Baker live in Rosenheim is a cool jazz album by Chet Baker from 1989. Among the tracks are "Funk in deep freeze" and "I'm a fool to want you".
